diagram_title: Authenticate User and Obtain API Token
purpose: Establish authenticated session with GitHub Copilot service — from OAuth device flow through token caching and alternative auth source detection

activities.name: Initiate Device Code Flow
activities.description: POST to github.com/login/device/code with client_id=Ov23ctDVkRmgkPke0Mmm, scope=read:user,read:org,repo,gist; returns device_code, user_code, verification_uri

activities.name: Poll for User Authorization
activities.description: POST to github.com/login/oauth/access_token with device_code, grant_type=urn:ietf:params:oauth:grant-type:device_code; poll at interval until user completes browser auth

activities.name: Fetch User Profile
activities.description: GET api.github.com/copilot_internal/user to obtain copilotUser object containing subscription tier, feature flags, and endpoint configuration

activities.name: Exchange GitHub Token for Copilot API Token
activities.description: Internal exchange converting GitHub OAuth access_token into capiSessionToken — a short-lived bearer token for LLM API calls

activities.name: Cache and Auto-Refresh Token
activities.description: Store capiSessionToken locally, monitor expiry timestamp, proactively refresh before expiration during active agent turns

activities.name: Detect Alternative Auth Sources
activities.description: Check environment variables (COPILOT_GITHUB_TOKEN > GH_TOKEN > GITHUB_TOKEN), HMAC key, and gh CLI credential store as alternative token sources

arrows.label: Feature flags (from copilotUser) gate auth behavior
Alternative token (env var or gh CLI credential)
Environment variables (COPILOT_GITHUB_TOKEN, GH_TOKEN, GITHUB_TOKEN, HMAC key)
Feature flags + endpoint config (controls model routing downstream)
